summary:
• Strait of Hormuz remains a critical chokepoint for global oil supply, with geopolitical tensions and shutdown risks driving crude prices higher despite efforts by alternative routes like UAE's Hormuz-bypass export capacity expansion.

• Argentina's Vaca Muerta shale play is emerging as a strategic supply alternative to Middle East oil, positioning the Western Hemisphere as increasingly important for global energy security amid persistent geopolitical risks.

• Canada's West Coast pipeline approval and rising US rig counts signal domestic production growth opportunities, while the Trump administration's SPR refill commitment further supports Western Hemisphere supply diversification away from conflict-prone regions.

articles.title: China Spent More on Clean Energy Than the Rest of the World Combined
articles.description: China has been outspending the rest of the world on clean energy for years now, and new data shows that the investment is paying off in spades. Beijing’s lead in the global clean energy race is wider than ever, according to new figures from the research firm Atlas Public Policy.
